detaileddescription.para: Default tunnel MTU (inner IP packet before encryption).
WireGuard transport overhead: type(4) + receiver(4) + counter(8) + tag(16) = 32
WireGuard protocol timers (seconds). Initiate rekey after this many seconds
Drop session after this many seconds
Retry handshake if no response (s)
Send keepalive if no outbound (s)
WireGuard message types. Handshake initiation message
Handshake response message
Cookie reply message
Encrypted transport message
Message sizes. Size of initiation message
Size of response message
No session, no handshake in progress
Sent initiation, waiting for response
Session active, transport data flowing

memberdef.argsstring: (SYN_WG *wg, const SYN_WgConfig *config, SYN_SNTP *sntp, uint8_t *rx_buf, size_t rx_buf_size, uint8_t *tx_buf, size_t tx_buf_size)
memberdef.name: syn_wg_init

detaileddescription.para: Derives the public key from the private key and prepares state. Does NOT open a socket or start a handshake — that happens when the protothread task runs.

parameterdescription.para: SNTP time source (must be initialized, may not be synced yet).

briefdescription.para: Send an IP packet through the WireGuard tunnel.

detaileddescription.para: Encrypts the packet with the current session key and sends it as a WireGuard transport message.

simplesect.para: SYN_OK on success, SYN_ERROR if no session or send failed.

briefdescription.para: Cooperative protothread task — drives the WireGuard client.

detaileddescription.para: Handles: NTP sync wait, handshake, incoming packet processing, keepalive, and rekeying. Pass the SYN_WG context via task->user_data.

briefdescription.para: Check if the tunnel is established and ready for data.

simplesect.para: true if the session is active.

briefdescription.para: WireGuard VPN client — Noise IK, pure C99, cooperative.

detaileddescription.para: Implements a WireGuard client as a cooperative protothread task. Handles the Noise_IKpsk2 handshake, ChaCha20-Poly1305 transport encryption, keepalive timers, and rekeying.
The client connects to a single WireGuard peer (server) over UDP. Decrypted IP packets are delivered to user code via a callback. User code sends packets into the tunnel via syn_wg_send().
No TUN device, no host routing — all traffic is handled in userspace. The host OS only sees a single UDP socket.
Requires: BLAKE2S, CHACHA20POLY1305, X25519, SNTP.
